author: Yike Wang, Huaisheng Zhu, Zhengyu Hu, Yige Yuan, Zhengyu Chen, Shakti Senthil, Hannaneh Hajishirzi, Yulia Tsvetkov, Pradeep Dasigi, Teng Xiao

readme.entry: - **[Rethinking the Evaluation of Harness Evolution for Agents](https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.12227)** — Yike Wang, Huaisheng Zhu, Zhengyu Hu, Yige Yuan, Zhengyu Chen, Shakti Senthil, Hannaneh Hajishirzi, Yulia Tsvetkov, Pradeep Dasigi, Teng Xiao — <https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.12227> · *research paper and reproducibility code* — A matched-budget Terminal-Bench study finds that evolved agent harnesses do not consistently outperform simpler test-time scaling or generalize reliably to held-out tasks. 🆕

factuality.reason: structured evidence plus fetched source snapshot

quality.reason: concrete eval artifacts or methodology signals present

recency.reason: evidence indicates 2025-2026 or actively updated source

section: ## 6 · Benchmark vs. eval (and benchmark integrity: contamination, saturation, label errors, leaderboard gaming)
source_seam: arXiv recent papers and agent-evaluation benchmark research
title: Rethinking the Evaluation of Harness Evolution for Agents

worker.candidate_reason: It directly audits a fast-growing evaluation claim with held-out tasks, matched feedback and inference budgets, frontier models, multiple search baselines, and a public repository containing the evaluation split, scripts, configurations, and outputs.
worker.evidence: The paper compares parallel sampling, sequential refinement, harness evolution, and harness scaling on held-out Terminal-Bench 2.1 tasks using GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 under matched feedback and inference budgets; its central result is that harness evolution does not consistently beat simple test-time scaling and shows limited held-out generalization. Code and experiment assets are published at https://github.com/rethinking-harness-evolution/code.
worker.summary: A matched-budget Terminal-Bench study finds that evolved agent harnesses do not consistently outperform simpler test-time scaling or generalize reliably to held-out tasks.
